payload.summary: Planned the extraction of gen-eval (~6.5 KLOC framework) from agent-coordinator/evaluation/gen_eval/ into packages/gen-eval/ as a standalone pip-installable package. Selected full cutover in a single atomic change, with framework-only data split, optional [mcp] extra, and an examples doc as the agentic-assistant adoption proof. The change establishes the packages/ convention for future shared-library extractions (Hoverfly, prompt opt, harness opt, vendor conventions).

decisions.title: Package lives at packages/gen-eval/ (new top-level convention)
decisions.rationale: User clarified that agentic-coding-tools is conceptually a coordinator service + shared component libraries, with multiple future extractions planned. Introducing the packages/ convention with one example is cheaper than retrofitting later with three. Rejected: evaluation/gen_eval/ at root (namespace ambiguity + single-purpose dir name); separate repo (highest setup cost, weakest coupling to OpenSpec workflow).

decisions.title: Framework-only data split; descriptors stay in consumers
decisions.rationale: Matches the stated architectural intent 'code in agentic-coding-tools, configuration in consumer repos'. Package ships .py + schemas/ + dtu/ templates + tests/fixtures/ + examples/. agent-coordinator keeps its descriptor.yaml + manifests/ + scenarios/ as project test fixtures.

decisions.title: Optional [mcp] extra inside the package
decisions.rationale: Two consumer profiles in the ecosystem: agent-coordinator wants MCP routing (it's a service), agentic-assistant wants pure Python (skills do orchestration). Optional extra is the only design that serves both without forcing a wrong dependency on either. mcp_service.py and clients/mcp_client.py wrap their fastmcp imports in try/except per design D4.

decisions.title: Move evaluation/metrics.py into the package
decisions.rationale: Resolves the only reverse coupling. GenEvalMetrics is named for gen-eval, only consumed by gen-eval's reports.py, and has no other consumers. Alternatives (Protocol injection, no-op stub, callback shim) all add indirection for zero gain.

decisions.title: Full cutover in one atomic change
decisions.rationale: Phased approach saves no work (the follow-up has the same skill/Dockerfile updates) and creates a drift window with two sources of truth. The convention isn't really set until the coordinator actually uses the package.

decisions.title: agentic-assistant adoption proven via examples doc only, not cross-repo PR
decisions.rationale: User chose the compromise option. Real cross-repo PR costs coordination overhead (separate CI, separate review cycle). The quickstart doc is verifiable inside this repo; if it turns out to be wrong, the agentic-assistant adoption will surface it.

next_steps: Start with wp-package-scaffold (priority 1) — create packages/gen-eval/ skeleton + pyproject + smoke test.
Then wp-framework-move (priority 2) — the big atomic move including metrics.py coupling resolution and mcp_service.py [mcp] extra wrap.
Fan out to four parallel packages: wp-coordinator-migrate, wp-package-tests, wp-skills-update, wp-examples-doc.
Converge at wp-integration: full validate-feature pass + Docker smoke.

relevant_files.description: 7 phases, 27 tasks with TDD ordering and spec/contract refs
Spec delta: 5 ADDED requirements + 1 MODIFIED requirement
7-package DAG, coordinated tier, 6 valid parallel pairs
